rag_rules: Place assume/guarantee/eq/const in component type annexes for interface behavior; use implementation annexes only for assign clauses tied to subcomponent bindings.
Use only visible feature names or explicitly declared constants/variables in AGREE expressions; avoid direct references to AADL properties.
generic_rules: Contract elements must reflect observable system interfaces, not internal implementation details or metadata.
Boolean logic in guarantees should encode necessary conditions using clear, composable expressions.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annexes require syntax: annex agree {** ... **};
Use const for named constants, eq for derived variables, assume for input constraints, guarantee for output claims.
anti_patterns: Do not embed AADL property names (e.g., Period, Deadline) as variables in AGREE expressions.
Do not reference subcomponent ports directly in component type contracts without intermediate variables or outputs.
